exercises.title: Inspect a certificate chain
exercises.description: Use openssl s_client to connect to a site and inspect the presented chain.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can see the leaf cert, intermediates, and negotiated cipher.

exercises.title: Compare HTTPS versions
exercises.description: Use curl to compare TLS behavior and protocol negotiation on a site.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can see TLS handshake details and the negotiated version.

exercises.title: Generate a local certificate
exercises.description: Create a self-signed certificate and serve a local HTTPS site with it.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can run a local HTTPS endpoint with a valid test cert file pair.

exercises.title: Inspect revocation behavior
exercises.description: Check whether a certificate advertises OCSP and compare with stapled responses.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can observe whether OCSP stapling is present.

exercises.title: Build an HTTPS server
exercises.description: Create a local HTTPS server and configure it to use a certificate and key.
exercises.expected_outcome: A browser can connect over HTTPS to your local server.

exercises.title: Compare TLS 1.2 and 1.3
exercises.description: Benchmark or at least inspect the handshake behavior of TLS 1.2 versus TLS 1.3 on the same endpoint.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can explain the handshake and round-trip differences.
